course.title: Build and Ship Accessible Asteroids with Python 3.14 and pygame-ce 2.5.7
course.description: Python and object-oriented programming learners who need to design, build, test, package, playtest, and defend a complete accessible real-time 2D game rather than copy a monolithic arcade tutorial. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7; sdl boundaries; initialization and game loops; vector2 and explicit units; variable and fixed time steps; events, held input, remapping, focus and controllers; scenes; ship physics; toroidal wrapping; responsive viewports; surfaces, draw order, transforms, and assets; projectiles; seeded asteroid geometry; layered collision detection and response; scoring, waves, safe spawns, and difficulty; sprites and groups; audio; accessible hud and menus; deterministic replay and saves; pure-core architecture; unit, property, headless, render, and playtest evidence; performance; pyinstaller 6.21 packaging; local security and privacy; release, rollback, recovery, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python evidence using original fixed fixtures plus explicit native pygame-ce, sdl, display, input, audio, asset, file, platform, profiler, package, accessibility playtest, performance, recovery, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic game deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-platform defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves player goal, revision, Python and pygame-ce versions, explicit units, seed, input and time trace, state invariant, bounded entities and work, accessible multichannel feedback, changed-case tests, failure and recovery evidence, and explicit browser-versus-desktop limits before adding one game boundary.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, asset, filesystem, GPU, frame-pacing, installer, or release behavior; those require controlled desktop evidence.

Passing work requires a stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate state evidence, at least one changed frame-rate, seam, input, collision, device, accessibility, or failure case, exact observable results, a test that detects a deliberate defect, and a named owner for remaining risk.

credential.title: Build and Ship Accessible Asteroids with Python 3.14 and pygame-ce 2.5.7 Certificate

modules.title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence
modules.description: A studio says “make Asteroids” but has not defined the player, verbs, success, failure, control needs, sensory alternatives, supported devices, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Learners cumulatively produce player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.

modules.title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop
modules.description: A script imports an incompatible pygame distribution, opens a window during import, ignores failed modules and quit events, presents twice, and never cleans up. Learners cumulatively produce owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.

modules.title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units
modules.description: The ship stores integer x and y, mixes degrees with radians, normalizes a zero vector, treats screen y as mathematical y, and adds pixels per second directly to position. Learners cumulatively produce unit-labeled 2D geometry model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.
Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.
Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.

modules.title: Clock, Delta Time, Fixed Steps, Interpolation, and Stalls
modules.description: Movement is tuned per frame, a long pause launches the ship across the map, collision results change with monitor refresh rate, and catch-up updates freeze the process. Learners cumulatively produce bounded deterministic time-step policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.
Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.
Accumulate real time into bounded fixed simulation updates and preserve remainder for interpolation.

modules.title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices
modules.description: The ship fires every frame a key is held, misses quit events, keeps thrust after focus loss, hard-codes WASD, assumes one controller identity, and requires precise simultaneous presses. Learners cumulatively produce device-independent remappable action layer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.
Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.

modules.title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership
modules.description: Menus, play, pause, death, and game over are booleans that form impossible combinations; pause leaves timers running; restart retains old projectiles and score. Learners cumulatively produce explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states.
Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.
Freeze simulation clocks and dangerous input while keeping menus, status, audio control, and window events responsive.

modules.title: Ship Rotation, Thrust, Inertia, Drag, and Speed Limits
modules.description: Thrust teleports position, diagonal motion is faster, velocity aliases facing, turning changes old momentum, drag depends on frames, and speed caps each component independently. Learners cumulatively produce frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction.
Integrate acceleration into velocity and velocity into position with explicit seconds and bounded time.
Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.

modules.title: Toroidal Wrapping, Object Bounds, Viewports, Resize, and Scaling
modules.description: Objects wrap when centers cross, disappear at seams, collide incorrectly across edges, resize teleports the world, high-DPI scaling distorts input, and zero-sized windows divide by zero. Learners cumulatively produce seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose center, radius, Rect, FRect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds.
Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.
Compare shortest toroidal displacement or ghost proxies so collisions remain continuous across seams.

modules.title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation
modules.description: Every frame reloads assets, repeatedly rotates transformed images, mixes alpha modes, draws UI behind the world, converts before display setup, and calls flip from several objects. Learners cumulatively produce owned layered rendering and asset pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
Define clear, background, world, effects, HUD, focus, diagnostics, and presentation order once per frame.
Rotate and scale from an immutable source, recenter output bounds, cache justified variants, and avoid cumulative degradation.

modules.title: Projectiles, Edge Triggers, Cooldowns, Lifetimes, and Reuse
modules.description: Held fire spawns unbounded bullets, cooldown uses frame count, expired bullets remain in collision groups, pool reuse keeps old velocity, and rapid restart leaks projectiles. Learners cumulatively produce bounded projectile lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Convert action edges and held auto-fire policy into deterministic fire requests separate from object creation.
Gate shots using simulation time, inclusive boundary tests, pause semantics, and a visible or audible readiness cue.
Derive muzzle position and velocity from ship transform while deciding whether ship momentum contributes.

modules.title: Seeded Asteroid Generation, Polygons, Sizes, and Difficulty Inputs
modules.description: Random points self-intersect, seeds are global and hidden, small rocks award less but move impossibly, spawns overlap the ship, and difficulty grows without bound. Learners cumulatively produce seeded readable asteroid factory through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.
Generate ordered angular vertices with bounded radius variation and validate minimum size, winding, and intersections.
Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.

modules.title: Broad Phase, Circle, Rect, Mask, Seam, and Swept Collision Detection
modules.description: The game tests every pair, uses transformed Rect overlap as exact geometry, constructs masks every frame, misses seam pairs, and lets fast bullets tunnel through small rocks. Learners cumulatively produce layered collision detection pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose circle, AABB, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation.
Use groups, spatial partitioning, sweep, or bounded pair sets to reduce candidates without dropping possible overlaps.
Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.

modules.title: Collision Events, Single Resolution, Fragments, Damage, and Lives
modules.description: One projectile damages the same rock twice, both loops delete the same sprite, fragments recursively collide during creation, ship damage repeats every frame, and feedback arrives after state removal. Learners cumulatively produce ordered single-resolution collision response through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate collision facts from response decisions so detection stays pure and effects occur in an owned phase.
Assign pair identity and terminal ownership so score, deletion, sound, fragments, and telemetry happen once.
Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.

modules.title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options
modules.description: Score drifts from destruction records, waves advance before fragments resolve, the ship respawns inside danger, pacing accelerates without respite, and assistance modes shame the player. Learners cumulatively produce fair progression and difficulty model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Advance only when active, queued, and pending-fragment counts satisfy a declared completion invariant.
Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.

modules.title: Sprites, Groups, Update Contracts, Kill Semantics, and Entity Ownership
modules.description: Sprites add themselves to global groups, update signatures differ, draw order depends on set iteration, killed objects keep external references, and restart doubles group membership. Learners cumulatively produce reconciled sprite and group lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define image, rect or FRect, world position, update inputs, draw responsibility, and lifecycle for every Sprite subtype.
Use separate update, draw-layer, collision, projectile, hazard, and UI groups only when ownership and membership are explicit.
Keep group update inputs consistent or route context through an explicit system boundary.

modules.title: Mixer Initialization, Channels, Latency, Volume, Mute, and Cue Alternatives
modules.description: Audio initializes late with wrong parameters, rapid shots cut off critical warnings, one slider controls everything, mute is unavailable, missing audio crashes launch, and gameplay meaning is sound-only. Learners cumulatively produce bounded accessible audio and cue system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits.
Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.
Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.

modules.title: HUD, Text, Menus, Focus, Multichannel Cues, and Motion Settings
modules.description: Tiny centered text scales poorly, focus is color-only, menus require a mouse, damage flashes rapidly, status disappears immediately, and reduced motion removes essential information. Learners cumulatively produce accessible responsive game interface and settings flow through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.
Support predictable keyboard and controller navigation, wrap policy, back, confirmation, cancellation, and no traps.
Render persistent non-color focus and announce or retain important state, errors, destructive actions, and setting changes.

modules.title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration
modules.description: Rendering consumes random draws, replay stores key state without timing, floats are compared as formatted text, settings overwrite atomically unsafe, and corrupted saves silently reset. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible replay and versioned local state through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.
Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.

modules.title: Pure Simulation Core, Effect Adapters, Events, and Dependency Direction
modules.description: Entities read global input, clock, screen, sound, and random state; constructors load files; collisions mutate UI; tests require a window; and modules import each other cyclically. Learners cumulatively produce testable game core and adapter architecture through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.
Keep display, events, devices, audio, assets, persistence, clock, logging, and packaging behind thin owned adapters.
Emit immutable collision, score, life, wave, cue, and transition records and consume them in explicit order.

modules.title: Unit, Property, Replay, Headless SDL, Render, and Playtest Evidence
modules.description: Tests assert implementation details, use real time and global random state, compare one screenshot, never inject failures, and call dummy video proof of usability. Learners cumulatively produce risk-layered automated and human game test suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Test vector, wrap, time, input, state, score, collision, spawn, and migration invariants with fixed fixtures.
Generate bounded cases for wrap, collision symmetry, conservation, reconciliation, replay, and invalid input and retain smallest failures.
Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.

modules.title: Frame Budgets, Profiling, Allocations, Collision Cost, and Responsiveness
modules.description: The team optimizes by feel, reports average FPS, profiles menus instead of peak play, allocates every draw, rebuilds masks per frame, and changes physics for speed. Learners cumulatively produce representative frame-time and capacity evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.
Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
Measure per-frame allocations and cache immutable assets, transforms, masks, text, or pools only when evidence justifies complexity.

modules.title: Project Metadata, Assets, PyInstaller 6.21, Platforms, and Clean Launch
modules.description: The game depends on the current directory, omits fonts and sound, builds one executable on Linux and calls it cross-platform, lacks licenses, and is never installed fresh. Learners cumulatively produce portable inspected game release candidate through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.
Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.

modules.title: Untrusted Assets, Save Parsing, Paths, Privacy, and Local Security
modules.description: A mod folder escapes its root, decompression consumes memory, pickle loads scores, save files are unlimited, crash reports include home paths, and telemetry starts without consent. Learners cumulatively produce bounded local-data and privacy policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences.
Constrain asset, settings, replay, and score paths to approved roots and reject traversal, symlink, type, and size violations.
Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.

modules.title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense
modules.description: A polished demo becomes release by copying the latest executable; no one owns accessibility findings, performance budgets, package provenance, crash recovery, rollback, or support. Learners cumulatively produce production game release and defense dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
